rows.Typical Evidence-Based Range: Contributes to daily protein target (file 2/7 Morton et al. 2018 ranges)
rows.Notes for System Output: Position as food-gap filler, not mandatory; flag lactose intolerance before recommending whey
Recommend consistent daily timing; note mild water retention is expected/benign; contraindicated if `medical_conditions` includes kidney disease — route to `medical_clearance_required` first
Flag if client reports anxiety/sleep issues or hypertension (file 12 Sec 4) — reduce/avoid; never stack with other stimulant pre-workouts without disclosure

rows.Typical Evidence-Based Range: As needed with heat/heavy sweat loss or fasted training
Only with disclosed deficiency or limited sun exposure
rows.Notes for System Output: System should recommend testing/physician guidance rather than blind supplementation

rows.Condition for Recommendation: Only if client has no cardiovascular/anxiety/sleep flags
rows.Flag/Caution: Contains stacked stimulants — never recommend alongside separate caffeine intake without total-dose awareness note
Harmless paresthesia (tingling) — inform client this is expected, not a reaction requiring stopping
Flag if on blood thinners (file 12 Section 5) — `bruising_bleeding_risk` overlaps, recommend physician check
Avoid stacking with other magnesium sources to prevent GI distress

rows.System Behavior: Do not recommend; if client asks, provide general caution about unregulated stimulant content and unverified label accuracy, redirect to Tier 1 approach (protein + deficit + activity)

Do not recommend, do not provide dosing information under any framing; state these are unregulated/unapproved for general use and carry unstudied risk; recommend physician consultation if client is already using them

System does not provide dosing, cycling, or PCT (post-cycle therapy) protocols under any framing, including "harm reduction." If client discloses current use, system should: (a) not judge or refuse basic training/nutrition support, (b) flag for coach awareness (`anabolic_use_disclosed`), (c) recommend physician-supervised bloodwork monitoring, (d) not adjust programming to assume enhanced recovery without explicit coach override
Same treatment as steroids — no dosing/protocol guidance, flag and recommend physician oversight

rows.Category: Ayurvedic/"proprietary blend" mass gainers with undisclosed ingredient amounts
rows.System Behavior: Caution that undisclosed proprietary blends prevent safe interaction checking; recommend products with full label transparency instead

rows.Flag: Avoid entirely per file 12 Section 4 pregnancy constraints

rows.Edge Case: Client asks for steroid cycle/PCT advice directly
rows.Rule: Decline to provide protocol; give the Section 3 physician-referral response; do not lecture repeatedly if client persists, restate once and redirect to what the system can help with

rows.Edge Case: Client already mid-cycle and wants training adjusted "to match"
rows.Rule: Do not proactively adjust volume/intensity assumptions for assumed enhanced recovery; log `anabolic_use_disclosed`, offer standard evidence-based programming, flag for coach awareness

rows.Edge Case: Client under 18 asks about any Tier 2/3 supplement
rows.Rule: Default to Tier 1 only recommendations; Tier 2/3 requires guardian/coach involvement given age overlay (file 12 Section 8)
Route entirely through file 12 Section 4 pregnancy constraints first; only Tier 1 electrolytes/vitamin D (with physician guidance) are ever considered, everything else deferred to physician

rows.Symptom: System recommending pre-workout to flagged hypertensive client
rows.Cause: Interaction matrix not queried before Tier 1/2 recommendation
rows.Fix: Enforce Section 4 lookup before any stimulant-containing suggestion

rows.Symptom: Client stacking 4+ new supplements at once with unclear symptom source
